name: Buenos Aires Province (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the sky over the Pampas; the national colour of Argentina
the wheat and grain harvests of the province

description: A horizontal bicolour of blue over green with, centred across the boundary, a yellow Sun of May surrounded by an open wreath of laurel branches and wheat ears, all the elements traced in yellow.
history: Adopted by provincial law 11997 on 9 December 1997 after a public competition won by Roberto Daniel Pez. The colours and emblem draw on the provincial coat of arms first used in the 19th century; blue and green were chosen to evoke the sky over the immense pampas and the fertility of the agricultural heartland of Argentina.

features.symbolism: blue for the sky and the colours of the Argentine flag; green for the fertile plains of the pampas

features.role: Sun of May radiating over the wheat-stalk wreath of the Province of Buenos Aires
features.symbolism: a stylised version of the provincial coat of arms: a Sun of May above two laurel branches, ringed by ears of wheat — the wheat for the agricultural wealth of the pampas, the laurels for the glory of the heroes of independence
